experiment_notes.winner_margin_vs_v07d2: 0.008 vs 0.034 — lower, but positive vs IL baseline (-0.004). High training variance due to torch.manual_seed not set. Categorical.sample() in game loop consumes different Torch RNG sequence each run.
experiment_notes.training_dynamics: iter20 wm=-0.029 (large excursion), iter29 wm=+0.008 (incomplete recovery). v07d2 may have had a more favorable random trajectory.
experiment_notes.infrastructure_result: FULLY VALIDATED: rl_episodes.pt (1.1GB, 292276 decisions, 30 iters x 200 games) written correctly and loadable. Cell[25] winner_margin criterion working — auto-generated decision no longer requires manual correction.
experiment_notes.model_result: BELOW THRESHOLD (0.008 < 0.030) but above IL baseline (-0.004). Not a regression caused by episode saving — Torch non-determinism is the source of variance.
experiment_notes.research_baseline_update: NOT updated — v07d3 wm=0.008 < v07d2 wm=0.034. v07d2 remains the research baseline.

known_issues: torch.manual_seed not set → RL runs are non-reproducible across versions
winner_margin variance is high (0.008 vs 0.034 across two identical-algorithm runs)
next_candidates: v07d4-a: load v07d3 rl_episodes.pt, run additional offline PPO epochs (test episode reuse, ~5 min)
v07d4-b: add torch.manual_seed(RANDOM_SEED) to stabilize training, re-run to measure true expected wm
v07d4-c: collect fresh episodes starting from v07d3 weights (warm start) to test curriculum continuation
